away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Ezi Magbegor
players.injury_comment: Magbegor sustained her injury while playing for Australia in the 2026 FIBA Basketball World Cup Qualifiers, and she appears to be on track to miss the start of the WNBA regular season as a result. The 26-year-old has played a prominent role in Seattle's frontcourt across the past several seasons, most recently averaging 8.0 points, 6.2 rebounds, 2.2 blocks and 2.1 assists in 27.3 minutes per game while starting every regular-season matchup in 2025, while her absence to begin the 2026 season could lead to increased opportunities for Dominique Malonga, Stefanie Dolson and rookie Awa Fam.

players.name: Taina Mair
players.injury_comment: Mair logged two minutes in Saturday's 88-68 loss to the Lynx. She is eligible to appear in 12 regular-season games, as she is signed to a developmental deal, so she won't play versus the Aces in an effort to preserve her eligibility moving ahead.

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Chennedy Carter
players.injury_comment: A left leg injury is keeping Carter sidelined for a third consecutive contest. Her next chance to play comes Thursday, when the Aces visit the Fire in Portland. Stephanie Talbot and Brianna Turner should help pick up the slack for Las Vegas on Monday

players.name: Dana Evans
players.injury_comment: Evans will remain on the inactive list due to a left leg injury, and she's still without a clear timetable for a return to action. Her next opportunity to play will come Thursday in Portland, though she can be considered doubtful for that contest until Las Vegas provides an update on her status.
